entry.name: ACTIVE_TEXT_FIELD_TYPE
entry.description_fallback: Subset of TextField types that render a browser-native picker overlay (color, file, time, date, datetime-local, week, month). Used internally to distinguish them from plain text inputs.
entry.definition:
export const ACTIVE_TEXT_FIELD_TYPE: Partial<Array<TTextFieldType>> = [
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.COLOR,
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.FILE,
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.TIME,
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.DATE,
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.DATETIME,
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.WEEK,
    TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.MONTH
]

examples.title_fallback: Detecting an active-picker input type
examples.code:
import { ACTIVE_TEXT_FIELD_TYPE } from 'origam'

const isActivePicker = (type: string) =>
  ACTIVE_TEXT_FIELD_TYPE.includes(type as never)

entry.name: ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES
entry.description_fallback: Per-tag attribute allowlist used by the rich Textarea HTML sanitiser. Only the declared attributes survive sanitisation; all others are stripped. Values are still validated against URL-scheme and class-prefix rules downstream.
entry.definition:
export const ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES: Readonly<Record<string, ReadonlyArray<string>>> = {
    a: ['href', 'class'],
    p: ['class'],
    ul: ['class'],
    ol: ['class'],
    li: ['class'],
    h1: ['class'],
    h2: ['class'],
    h3: ['class'],
    code: ['class'],
    strong: ['class'],
    em: ['class'],
    u: ['class'],
    b: ['class'],
    i: ['class']
}

examples.title_fallback: Checking which attributes are kept for a tag
examples.code:
import { ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES } from 'origam'

const kept = ALLOWED_ATTRIBUTES['a'] // ['href', 'class']

entry.name: ALLOWED_CLASS_PREFIX
entry.description_fallback: Whitelist prefix for class attributes that survive the rich Textarea HTML sanitiser. Only classes starting with this prefix are retained, preventing arbitrary class injection while allowing semantic rich-text classes.

examples.title_fallback: Validating a class against the allowed prefix

entry.name: ALLOWED_TAGS
entry.description_fallback: Tags retained by the rich Textarea HTML sanitiser. Anything outside this allowlist is unwrapped (children preserved, parent tag dropped). Scripts and dangerous containers are instead fully removed via BLOCKED_TAGS.

examples.title_fallback: Checking if a tag is allowed

entry.name: ALLOWED_URL_SCHEMES
entry.description_fallback: URL schemes allowed on <a href> values in the rich Textarea sanitiser. Tested against the start of the trimmed, lower-cased href. Anything else (notably javascript:, data:, vbscript:) is stripped.

examples.title_fallback: Validating a URL scheme
examples.code:
import { ALLOWED_URL_SCHEMES } from 'origam'

const isSafeUrl = (href: string) =>
  ALLOWED_URL_SCHEMES.some(s => href.trim().toLowerCase().startsWith(s))

entry.name: BCO
entry.description_fallback: Blue channel luminance coefficient used in the WCAG relative-luminance formula (IEC 61966-2-1). Combined with RCO and GCO to compute perceptual luminance from linearised RGB values.

examples.title_fallback: Computing WCAG relative luminance

entry.name: BLK_CLMP
entry.description_fallback: Soft-clamp factor applied to near-black values in the APCA contrast algorithm. Prevents the luminance of very dark colours from reaching absolute zero, which would produce infinite contrast ratios.

examples.title_fallback: Applying the black clamp in APCA luminance
examples.code:
import { BLK_THRS, BLK_CLMP } from 'origam'

// APCA black-clamp: if Y < BLK_THRS → Y = Y + (BLK_THRS - Y) ** BLK_CLMP
const clamp = (Y: number) =>
  Y < BLK_THRS ? Y + (BLK_THRS - Y) ** BLK_CLMP : Y

entry.name: BLK_THRS
entry.description_fallback: Black-clamp threshold used in the APCA contrast algorithm. Luminance values below this threshold are soft-clamped with BLK_CLMP to avoid division-by-zero and infinite-contrast artefacts.

examples.title_fallback: Applying the black clamp in APCA luminance
examples.code:
import { BLK_THRS, BLK_CLMP } from 'origam'

const clamp = (Y: number) =>
  Y < BLK_THRS ? Y + (BLK_THRS - Y) ** BLK_CLMP : Y

entry.name: BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX
entry.description_fallback: Regular expression used to detect a free-form CSS border-radius string passed via the rounded prop. Accepts 1–4 values with standard CSS length units or a unit-less 0. Non-matching values fall through to the named-variant or boolean branches of useRounded.

examples.title_fallback: Testing if a value is a free-form radius
examples.code:
import { BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX } from 'origam'

BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX.test('4px')         // true
BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX.test('4px 0 4px 0') // true
BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX.test('lg')           // false → named variant

entry.name: BORDER_REGEX
entry.description_fallback: Regular expression that parses a free-form border shorthand value into named capture groups: width, style, and color. Supports hex literals, CSS functions (rgb/hsl/rgba), CSS custom properties (var(--…)), and named keywords. The var() alternative is placed before the bare [A-Za-z]+ branch to avoid eating "var" as a keyword.

examples.title_fallback: Parsing a border shorthand value

entry.name: BRACKET_DEFAULT_LOSERS_LABEL
entry.description_fallback: Default heading rendered above the loser-bracket tree in a double-elimination layout. Override via the losersLabel prop (e.g. to localise). Kept as a literal in withDefaults, mirrored here for consumer reference.

examples.title_fallback: Overriding the losers label

entry.name: BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_GAP
entry.description_fallback: Default vertical gap in pixels between sibling matches in the first round of an OrigamBracket. Subsequent rounds double the gap to produce the classic branching pattern. Mirrors the bracket.gap-round design token.

examples.title_fallback: Computing the gap for a given round
examples.code:
import { BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_GAP } from 'origam'

// Gap doubles each round: round 0 → 24px, round 1 → 48px, etc.
const gapForRound = (round: number) =>
  BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_GAP * Math.pow(2, round)

entry.name: BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_HEIGHT
entry.description_fallback: Default match card height in pixels used by the OrigamBracket layout algorithm when computing SVG connector paths in horizontal mode. Mirrors the bracket.match.height design token; duplicated in code so the connector pure function stays free of DOM measurement.

examples.title_fallback: Using the default height in a layout calculation
examples.code:
import { BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_HEIGHT } from 'origam'

// Compute the vertical midpoint of a match card
const midY = (rowIndex: number) =>
  rowIndex * (BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_HEIGHT + gap) + BRACKET_DEFAULT_MATCH_HEIGHT / 2

entry.description_fallback: Default match card width (in px) used by the OrigamBracket layout algorithm when computing connector paths in horizontal mode. Mirrors the token bracket.match.width.

examples.title_fallback: Overriding the default match width

entry.name: CHART_Y_TICK_COUNT
entry.description_fallback: Default number of Y-axis ticks drawn by OrigamChart. The composable snaps the data range to a nice multiple of this value — five rows fits most narrow chart heights without crowding.

examples.title_fallback: Referencing CHART_Y_TICK_COUNT for custom axis logic

entry.name: CIELAB_FORWARD_TRANSFORM
entry.description_fallback: CIELAB forward transfer function f(t). Converts a linearised XYZ tristimulus value to the CIE L*a*b* cube-root compressed domain. Threshold determined by COLOR_DELTA (6÷29).
entry.value: (t: number): number => ( t > COLOR_DELTA ** 3 ? Math.cbrt(t) : (t / (3 * COLOR_DELTA ** 2)) + 4 / 29 )

examples.title_fallback: Applying the forward transform in a color conversion

entry.name: CIELAB_REVERSE_TRANSFORM
entry.description_fallback: CIELAB reverse transfer function f⁻¹(t). Converts a CIE L*a*b* cube-root-compressed value back to a linearised XYZ tristimulus. Threshold determined by COLOR_DELTA (6÷29).
entry.value: (t: number): number => ( t > COLOR_DELTA ? t ** 3 : (3 * COLOR_DELTA ** 2) * (t - 4 / 29) )

examples.title_fallback: Applying the reverse transform in Lab→XYZ conversion

entry.name: COLOR_ACTIVE_MIX_PCT
entry.description_fallback: Percentage of black mixed into the rest-state background color to produce the active (pressed) state. Used as a math-fallback when the designer-tuned bgActive token is absent.

examples.title_fallback: Computing the active darken mix manually

entry.name: COLOR_DELTA
entry.description_fallback: CIE delta constant (6÷29 ≈ 0.2069) used as the threshold and scaling factor in CIELAB_FORWARD_TRANSFORM and CIELAB_REVERSE_TRANSFORM. Derived from the 6/29 ratio defined in the CIE 1976 standard.

examples.title_fallback: Using COLOR_DELTA as the CIE Lab threshold
examples.code:
import { COLOR_DELTA } from 'origam'

// Reproduce the forward piecewise function
const f = (t: number) =>
  t > COLOR_DELTA ** 3 ? Math.cbrt(t) : t / (3 * COLOR_DELTA ** 2) + 4 / 29

entry.name: COLOR_DELTA_Y_MIN
entry.description_fallback: Minimum luminance delta (Y) for APCA contrast math. Values below this threshold are clamped to avoid division-by-zero in the contrast-ratio pipeline.

examples.title_fallback: Clamping luminance before APCA division
examples.code:
import { COLOR_DELTA_Y_MIN } from 'origam'

const safeLuminance = (y: number) =>
  Math.max(y, COLOR_DELTA_Y_MIN)

entry.name: COLOR_HOVER_MIX_PCT
entry.description_fallback: Percentage of black mixed into the rest-state background color to derive the hover surface. Used as math fallback when no designer-tuned bgHover token exists.

examples.title_fallback: Computing a hover color via color-mix fallback

entry.name: COLOR_MAPPERS
entry.description_fallback: Record of parser functions keyed by CSS color-function name (rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla, hsv, hsva). Each function converts its channel arguments into an RGBA object, normalising via HSLtoRGB or HSVtoRGB where needed.
entry.definition:
export const COLOR_MAPPERS = {
    rgb: (r: number, g: number, b: number, a?: number) => ({r, g, b, a}),
    rgba: (r: number, g: number, b: number, a?: number) => ({r, g, b, a}),
    hsl: (h: number, s: number, l: number, a?: number) => HSLtoRGB({h, s, l, a}),
    hsla: (h: number, s: number, l: number, a?: number) => HSLtoRGB({h, s, l, a}),
    hsv: (h: number, s: number, v: number, a?: number) => HSVtoRGB({h, s, v, a}),
    hsva: (h: number, s: number, v: number, a?: number) => HSVtoRGB({h, s, v, a})
}

values.value: rgb: (r: number, g: number, b: number, a?: number) => ({r, g, b, a})
rgba: (r: number, g: number, b: number, a?: number) => ({r, g, b, a})
hsl: (h: number, s: number, l: number, a?: number) => HSLtoRGB({h, s, l, a})
hsla: (h: number, s: number, l: number, a?: number) => HSLtoRGB({h, s, l, a})
hsv: (h: number, s: number, v: number, a?: number) => HSVtoRGB({h, s, v, a})

entry.name: COUNTRY_CENTROIDS
entry.description_fallback: Visual centroid [x, y] coordinates for each country on the 1000 × 500 Mercator canvas used by WORLD_MAP_PATHS. Values are approximate geographic centres of each country shape and serve as endpoints for Bezier flight-route curves.

entry.name: CUSTOM_BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX
entry.description_fallback: Regex that detects free-form CSS border-radius values not covered by the fixed-unit BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX whitelist: custom-property references (var(…)), CSS math functions (calc/clamp/min/max/env), and single lengths with viewport or advanced units (vw, vh, lh, …). import { CUSTOM_BORDER_RADIUS_REGEX } from 'origam'

isCustom('sm')                          // false → falls to named variant

entry.name: LIST_OPEN_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: Open strategy for list-style navigation (e.g. OrigamList). Uses MULTIPLE_OPEN_STRATEGY for opening and a custom select handler that closes all siblings not on the path from the selected item to the root.

values.value: select: ({id, value, opened, parents}) => { if (!value) return opened const path: Array<unknown> = [] let parent = parents.get(id) while (parent != null) { path.push(parent) parent = parents.get(parent) } return new Set(path) }

entry.name: LO_CLIP
entry.description_fallback: APCA minimum luminance clipping value (0.001). Values below this threshold are treated as absolute black to avoid mathematical artefacts in the contrast ratio calculation.

examples.title_fallback: Applying the luminance clip in an APCA calculation
examples.code:
import { LO_CLIP } from 'origam'

const clampedLuminance = (L: number) => Math.max(L, LO_CLIP)

entry.name: LO_CON_FACTOR
entry.description_fallback: APCA low-contrast region scaling factor (≈ 12.82). Applied in the low-contrast branch of the Lc calculation when the absolute polarity-adjusted contrast falls below LO_CON_THRESH, to linearise the output near zero.

examples.title_fallback: Internal APCA usage (informational)
examples.code:
import { LO_CON_FACTOR } from 'origam'

// Internal — used inside the APCA Lc computation
const linearisedContrast = (rawLc: number) =>
  (rawLc - LO_CON_OFFSET) * LO_CON_FACTOR

entry.name: LO_CON_OFFSET
entry.description_fallback: Offset applied to low-contrast APCA calculations to prevent clamping near zero. Part of the SAPC/APCA perceptual contrast algorithm used by useContrast.

examples.title_fallback: APCA low-contrast offset in context

entry.name: LO_CON_THRESH
entry.description_fallback: Threshold below which an APCA contrast value is considered too low to be meaningful and is clamped to zero. Part of the SAPC/APCA perceptual contrast algorithm used by useContrast.

examples.title_fallback: APCA low-contrast threshold in context
examples.code:
import { LO_CON_THRESH, LO_CON_OFFSET } from 'origam'

// Absolute APCA contrast below LO_CON_THRESH is clamped to 0.
const clamp = (apca: number) =>
  Math.abs(apca) < LO_CON_THRESH ? 0 : apca - Math.sign(apca) * LO_CON_OFFSET

entry.name: MAGIC_RADIUS
entry.description_fallback: Radius (in SVG user units) of the circle path used by OrigamProgress circular mode. The value 20 is chosen so that CIRCUMFERENCE = 2π×20 ≈ 125.66, which maps cleanly to a 0–100 stroke-dashoffset range.

examples.title_fallback: Computing the SVG circle circumference

entry.name: MAIN_TRC
entry.description_fallback: Gamma exponent (Tone Response Curve) for the sRGB linearisation used in APCA contrast calculations. The value 2.4 is the IEC 61966-2-1 standard exponent for sRGB.

entry.name: MEDIA_DEFAULT_VOLUME
entry.description_fallback: Default volume (0–1 range) for OrigamAudio and OrigamVideo. Value 1 means full volume, matching the browser native default. Materialised here so useMediaPlayer, OrigamAudio and OrigamVideo share a single source of truth instead of re-typing the literal.

entry.name: MULTIPLE_OPEN_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: Open strategy that allows any number of nested items to be expanded simultaneously. When opening an item all its ancestors are also opened; when closing only the targeted item is removed from the opened set. Contrast with SINGLE_OPEN_STRATEGY which collapses siblings. Used by OrigamList, OrigamTreeview and any nested component.

values.value: open: ({id, value, opened, parents}) => { if (value) { let parent = parents.get(id) opened.add(id) while (parent != null && parent !== id) { opened.add(parent) parent = parents.get(parent) } return opened } else { opened.delete(id) } return opened }

values.description_fallback: Selection is not tracked by this strategy (returns null).

entry.name: NORM_BG
entry.description_fallback: APCA normalisation exponent for background luminance. Used in the SAPC/APCA perceptual contrast algorithm to scale the background term before computing the signed contrast difference.

examples.title_fallback: APCA background normalisation
examples.code:
import { NORM_BG, NORM_TXT } from 'origam'

// Sapc/Apca signed contrast (simplified)
const scaledBg  = linearBg  ** NORM_BG
const scaledTxt = linearTxt ** NORM_TXT
const contrast  = scaledBg - scaledTxt

entry.name: NORM_TXT
entry.description_fallback: APCA normalisation exponent for text luminance. Used in the SAPC/APCA perceptual contrast algorithm to scale the text-colour term before computing the signed contrast difference.

examples.title_fallback: APCA text normalisation alongside NORM_BG
examples.code:

const scaledBg  = linearBg  ** NORM_BG   // 0.55
const scaledTxt = linearTxt ** NORM_TXT  // 0.58

entry.name: OPPOSITE_MAP

export const OPPOSITE_MAP = {
    center: 'center',
    top: 'bottom',
    bottom: 'top',
    left: 'right',
    right: 'left',
    start: 'right',
    end: 'left'
}

values.description_fallback: start (logical) maps to the physical right in the default LTR direction.
end (logical) maps to the physical left in the default LTR direction.

examples.title_fallback: Flipping a placement when out of bounds
examples.code:
import { OPPOSITE_MAP } from 'origam'

type TLocation = keyof typeof OPPOSITE_MAP

function flip(location: TLocation): TLocation {
  return OPPOSITE_MAP[location] as TLocation
}

flip('top')    // 'bottom'
flip('left')   // 'right'

entry.description_fallback: The list of allowed density values (default, compact, comfortable) used to validate and iterate the density prop across components.
entry.definition:
export const PREDEFINED_DENSITY: Array<TDensity> = [
    DENSITY.DEFAULT,
    DENSITY.COMPACT,
    DENSITY.COMFORTABLE
]

values.description_fallback: Standard padding — balanced for most contexts.
Reduced padding — useful in tables and toolbars.
Increased padding — comfortable for forms and cards.

examples.title_fallback: Validating a density value
examples.code:
import { PREDEFINED_DENSITY } from 'origam'

const isValid = (value: string) =>
  PREDEFINED_DENSITY.includes(value as never)

entry.definition:
export const PREDEFINED_ROUNDED: Array<TRounded> = [
    ROUNDED.X_SMALL,
    ROUNDED.SMALL,
    ROUNDED.DEFAULT,
    ROUNDED.MEDIUM,
    ROUNDED.LARGE,
    ROUNDED.X_LARGE,
    ROUNDED.SHAPED,
    ROUNDED.SHAPED_INVERT
]

values.description_fallback: Shaped invert — asymmetric rounding in the opposite direction.

entry.name: QR_CODE_DEFAULT_LOGO_PADDING

examples.title_fallback: Applying the default logo padding

entry.name: QR_CODE_DEFAULT_LOGO_SIZE
entry.description_fallback: Default logo overlay size as a ratio of the QR code width — 0.2 (20%) is the recommended ceiling before error correction starts failing.

examples.title_fallback: Sizing a logo overlay relative to the QR width

entry.name: QR_CODE_DEFAULT_MARGIN
entry.description_fallback: Default quiet-zone width in modules surrounding the QR matrix — ISO/IEC 18004 recommends 4 modules; smaller values significantly hurt scanner reliability.

examples.title_fallback: Referencing the default quiet-zone width

entry.name: QR_CODE_LRU_CAPACITY
entry.description_fallback: Maximum number of entries kept in the module-level LRU cache keyed on serialised payload + options — 16 entries covers a realistic storybook scenario (value variants × ECC levels × small tweaks).

examples.title_fallback: Understanding the LRU cache size

entry.name: QR_CODE_OVERLAY_MAX_RATIO
entry.description_fallback: Maximum safe overlay size as a ratio of the QR width — above 0.3 (30%) even error-correction level 'H' cannot reconstruct the obscured codewords and scans start to fail. The composable warns instead of clamping so the consumer keeps artistic control.

examples.title_fallback: Validating overlay ratio before rendering

entry.name: QUOTE_MARKS_BY_LANG
entry.description_fallback: Map of locale codes to decorative opening and closing quote glyphs used by OrigamBlockquote. The 'auto' key is resolved at runtime from document.documentElement.lang; missing languages fall back to the 'en' pair.

values.description_fallback: French guillemets with non-breaking thin spaces.
German low-9 opening quote and curly closing quote.

examples.title_fallback: Wrapping a citation in locale-aware quotes
examples.code:
import { QUOTE_MARKS_BY_LANG } from 'origam'

const lang = 'fr'
const { open, close } = QUOTE_MARKS_BY_LANG[lang]
console.log(`${open}Hello${close}`) // « Hello »

entry.description_fallback: Red channel luminance coefficient (0.2126729) in the sRGB-to-relative-luminance formula (WCAG 2.x). Used with GCO and BCO by color utilities to compute contrast ratios and APCA lightness.

examples.title_fallback: Computing relative luminance from linearised RGB

entry.description_fallback: Regular expression that validates an ISO 8601 date string in YYYY-MM-DD format (years 1000–2999, months 1–12, days 1–31). Used by date utilities to guard against malformed inputs before parsing.

examples.title_fallback: Validating a date string before parsing
examples.code:

const isValid = (s: string) => REGEX_DATE_YYYY_MM_DD.test(s)

entry.name: SHEET_FAST_FLICK_THRESHOLD
entry.description_fallback: Velocity threshold (in pixels per millisecond) above which a swipe release on a BottomSheet is treated as a "fast flick". When exceeded, the sheet snaps one step in the direction of motion instead of snapping to the nearest position by distance. 0.5 px/ms ≈ a 200 px swipe in 400 ms.

examples.title_fallback: Detecting a fast flick in a custom swipe handler
examples.code:
import { SHEET_FAST_FLICK_THRESHOLD } from 'origam'

const velocityPxPerMs = deltaY / deltaTime
const isFastFlick = Math.abs(velocityPxPerMs) > SHEET_FAST_FLICK_THRESHOLD

entry.name: SINGLE_OPEN_STRATEGY
entry.description_fallback: Nested-open strategy that enforces a single-open-at-a-time policy: opening a node closes any previously open sibling, while parents of the newly opened node are kept open. The `select` hook is a no-op (returns null). Used as the default strategy for ExpansionPanel.

values.value: open: ({id, value, opened, parents}) => { if (value) { const newOpened = new Set<unknown>() newOpened.add(id) let parent = parents.get(id) while (parent != null) { newOpened.add(parent) parent = parents.get(parent) } return newOpened } else { opened.delete(id) return opened } }

examples.title_fallback: Passing the strategy to an OrigamExpansionPanels
examples.code:
<template>
  <origam-expansion-panels :open-strategy="SINGLE_OPEN_STRATEGY">
    <origam-expansion-panel title="Panel A">Content A</origam-expansion-panel>
    <origam-expansion-panel title="Panel B">Content B</origam-expansion-panel>
  </origam-expansion-panels>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { SINGLE_OPEN_STRATEGY } from 'origam'
</script>

entry.name: SRGB_FORWARD_MATRIX
entry.description_fallback: XYZ-to-sRGB linear transformation matrix (3×3) used in the color pipeline to convert CIE XYZ tristimulus values to linear sRGB channel values before gamma expansion via SRGB_FORWARD_TRANSFORM.

values.description_fallback: Red channel row of the XYZ→sRGB matrix.
Green channel row of the XYZ→sRGB matrix.
Blue channel row of the XYZ→sRGB matrix.

examples.title_fallback: Applying the XYZ→sRGB matrix (internal use)

entry.name: SRGB_FORWARD_TRANSFORM
entry.description_fallback: sRGB gamma-expansion function (linear → sRGB encoded). Applies the IEC 61966-2-1 piecewise formula: values ≤ 0.0031308 are linearised as C × 12.92; values above use the power-curve branch 1.055 × C^(1/2.4) − 0.055.

examples.title_fallback: Converting a linear channel value to sRGB
examples.code:
import { SRGB_FORWARD_TRANSFORM } from 'origam'

// Linearised red channel → sRGB encoded
const linearR = 0.5
const encodedR = SRGB_FORWARD_TRANSFORM(linearR) // ~0.7354

entry.name: SRGB_REVERSE_MATRIX
entry.description_fallback: sRGB-to-XYZ linear transformation matrix (3×3) used in the color pipeline to convert linear sRGB channel values to CIE XYZ tristimulus values. Paired with SRGB_REVERSE_TRANSFORM for the sRGB → XYZ direction.

values.description_fallback: X channel row of the sRGB→XYZ matrix.
Y channel row (luminance) of the sRGB→XYZ matrix.
Z channel row of the sRGB→XYZ matrix.

examples.title_fallback: Applying the sRGB→XYZ matrix (internal use)

entry.name: SRGB_REVERSE_TRANSFORM
entry.description_fallback: sRGB gamma-compression function (sRGB encoded → linear). Applies the IEC 61966-2-1 piecewise decode: values ≤ 0.04045 are linearised as C / 12.92; values above use the power-curve branch ((C + 0.055) / 1.055)^2.4.

examples.title_fallback: Decoding an sRGB channel value to linear
examples.code:
import { SRGB_REVERSE_TRANSFORM } from 'origam'

// sRGB-encoded red channel → linearised value for matrix multiplication
const encodedR = 0.8
const linearR = SRGB_REVERSE_TRANSFORM(encodedR) // ~0.6038
